description: Fingerprint log for EdgeCase deduplication. Before indexing, match mediaFingerprint or incidentKey. Prefer original dashcam author over news/aggregator. Never make RT/quote canonical when an original video exists.
rules: Match mediaFingerprint (Twitter media/amplify id) — exact same file/encode family.
Match incidentKey — same real-world event even if re-uploaded/re-encoded by media outlets.
Canonical chooser: has video > owner/non-aggregator > older post > not RT/quote.
Aliases stay in video-log only; site embeds the canonical postUrl.
Render thumbnails with referrerPolicy=no-referrer so pbs.twimg.com loads on GitHub Pages.

entries.summary: Foggy railroad near-miss: driver intervenes late as the vehicle fails to stop for a train / crossing.
West Covina railroad crossing: FSD drives through a lowered gate at ~23 mph as a train approaches.
FSD 14.3 reacts to a reckless cut-in / aggressive Texas driver — owner credits the system with a save.
Controlled test: FSD brakes and swerves to avoid a pedestrian dummy thrown into its path at ~45 mph.
Claimed sub-human reaction timing (67ms) in a pedestrian-stop demo on FSD 14.2.1.
FSD 14.3 compilation of rapid reactions around vulnerable road users darting into the roadway.
FSD 14.3 handles people darting into the road — strong VRU performance clip.
Low-light stress test: FSD 14.3 pedestrian detection passes; ravine detection called out as a fail in the same thread.
FSD detects a police-blocked intersection and reroutes around closed lanes.
FSD 14.3 completes an unprotected left the instant the left blinker engages — reaction-time demo.
FSD 14.3 path planner reacts to a yellow light before the on-screen visualization catches up.
Compilation framing: AVs (incl. Tesla FSD) stopping for animals vs human-driven wildlife strikes.
FSD hesitates for ~a minute leaving a parallel parking spot despite clear gaps — awkward but low severity.
FSD v14.3.4 Mad Max mode navigating dense LA traffic with assertive lane behavior.
Tesla owners have now driven >8 billion miles on FSD Supervised
The Tesla Model Ys rolling off the production line at Giga Berlin have now driven themselves on FSD a combined 93,000 miles from the end of the production line to the outbound lot.
Mountain / winding road drive completed on FSD that the owner usually prefers to drive manually.
Self-Driving intervention reasons menu: “Other” replaced with “Navigation.”
Updated FSD intervention / disengagement feedback popup — smaller, less blocking after takeover.
Official Tesla promo: FSD Supervised can drive you where you want — marketing demo, not an edge stress case.
Owner notes smoother lane changes on FSD 14.3.4 after a minor point-release.
Actually Smart Summon response time on FSD v14.3.2 is instant.
Actually Smart Summon on FSD v14.3.2 pulls over to the curb when it’s done like a Robotaxi does.
FSD v14 lite drives me from Culver City to Hollywood with zero interventions, including parking perfectly at the Tesla Diner supercharger. @Tesla_AI

FSD v14.2.1 perfectly handles all lanes of the freeway merging into one after a closure due to the ending of a police chase/accident.

🚨 Here’s Tesla Full Self-Driving v14.1 navigating to a store, pulling in, choosing a parking spot, and backing right in From v13.2.9, this is a drastic improvement. Typically, manual parking was required in past…

🚨 Tesla Full Self-Driving v14.1 travels at more realistic speeds on local roads. With 13.2.9, even on Hurry, it would hover the speed limit a little too much, often times traveling 1-2 MPH below or over. It now…

Bug 🐞 no. 2: Navigation clearly shows the route continuing straight through the traffic light. I noticed the right turn signal coming on, so I overrode it. The car turned right anyway. I took over and drove manually…

🚨 Here, @Tesla Full Self-Driving v14.1 searched for a parking space at the grocery store. It did one loop, navigating safely through pedestrians and carts before it decided this spot was good enough for me. This…

🚨 Check out Tesla Full Self-Driving v14 choosing and backing into a Supercharger After selecting this Supercharger at the beginning of my trip, my Tesla had already selected “Charger” as the arrival option

Now for a couple bugs 🐞 Tesla FSD v14.1 put its right turn signal on as if it was going to pull over. It did move closer to curb, but then realized this wasn’t the correct maneuver for our route. It proceeded without…
Not only can FSD 14.1 pull into my gravel driveway (no version before has done this) it can pull into the garage.

FSD v14 navigates parking garages really well. Here’s a full clip of it leaving a parking spot, going through the garage, waiting for me to put the ticket in, and exiting. Almost feels like it can read the signs…
